methodsAttempted.query: site:kku.ac.th Khon Kaen University artificial intelligence policy AI academic integrity teaching generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found an official KKU Education and Digital Division AI literacy framework page, an official Graduate School PDF announcement on thesis and independent study AI use, and multiple news/event pages.
Firecrawl search found additional official KKU news pages referencing AI strategy and events, but not a central university-wide binding AI policy page.

methodsAttempted.query: official KKU subdomain pages and PDFs on AI disclosure, AI literacy, academic ethics, and thesis format
methodsAttempted.notes: Direct official fetches checked robots endpoints, the Graduate School PDF headers, the Education and Digital Division page, and KKU news pages. Missing robots.txt on checked hosts was treated as no explicit disallow, not as permission to bypass controls.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found one substantive Graduate School announcement PDF governing plagiarism prevention and AI use in theses or independent studies, plus a KKU Education and Digital Division AI literacy framework page supporting a teaching-guidance claim. KKU central news pages about AI seminars, showcases, and future-university strategy were documented but rejected as event/news coverage rather than binding policy evidence.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Graduate School, Khon Kaen University Announcement No. 173/2025 on Thesis and Independent Study Format, Edition 3
Empowering Our Students for an AI-Driven Future: The AI Literacy Framework at Khon Kaen University

artifacts.claimText: Khon Kaen University's Graduate School states that AI is only a supporting tool for thesis or independent study work, and students who use AI must specify the type and content of the AI use in the thesis or independent study report.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Graduate School thesis or independent study AI use requires disclosure of AI type and content; AI is described as a supporting tool.

artifacts.claimText: Khon Kaen University's Graduate School states that failure to disclose AI use in a thesis or independent study is regarded as a violation of academic ethics and may be subject to penalties under relevant regulations.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Undisclosed AI use in Graduate School thesis or independent study work is treated as an academic ethics violation and may be penalized.

citation.citationTitle: Graduate School, Khon Kaen University Announcement No. 173/2025 on Thesis and Independent Study Format, Edition 3

artifacts.claimText: Khon Kaen University's Graduate School classifies proofreading, grammar or spelling correction, readability rephrasing without altering core content, and translation for comprehension or cross-language communication as low-risk acceptable AI uses in thesis or independent study preparation, provided the use is disclosed.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Low-risk acceptable AI uses listed by the Graduate School require disclosure but not prior approval.

citation.citationTitle: Graduate School, Khon Kaen University Announcement No. 173/2025 on Thesis and Independent Study Format, Edition 3
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Khon Kaen University's Graduate School allows moderate-risk AI use in thesis or independent study work only when explicitly disclosed and supervised by the advisor, while strictly prohibiting high-risk AI use that generates entirely new content in place of student-authored writing.
artifacts.normalizedValue: Moderate-risk AI use is conditional on disclosure and advisor supervision; de novo AI-generated thesis content replacing student writing is prohibited.

artifacts.claimText: Khon Kaen University's Education and Digital Division describes an AI literacy framework for undergraduate success that includes technical proficiency, critical thinking, creative problem-solving, ethical awareness, and inclusive participation, with strategic implementation areas including educational integration, faculty development, and an ethical framework.
artifacts.normalizedValue: KKU Education and Digital Division AI literacy framework for undergraduate teaching and faculty development.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Graduate School, Khon Kaen University Announcement No. 173/2025 on Thesis and Independent Study Format, Edition 3
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Since Artificial Intelligence (AI) is merely a supporting tool for thesis or independent study, the user is responsible for the accuracy of the information and the work produced. If AI is utilized in supporting the preparation of a thesis or independent study, the student must specify the type and content of the AI usage within the thesis or independent study report.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Level 1: Low-risk-Acceptable Use: Permissible without prior approval, but the usage must be disclosed in the "Statement on the Use of Artificial Intelligence". 1. Proofreading and correction of English grammar and spelling. 2. Rephrasing sentences or words to improve readability (without altering the core content). 3. Translation for the purpose of comprehension or cross-language communication.

artifacts.title: Khon Kaen University AI Policy Evidence Snapshot
artifacts.summary: Staged evidence for Khon Kaen University is strongest for Graduate School thesis and independent study AI-use disclosure, academic ethics, and risk-level guidance. A KKU Education and Digital Division page supports a narrower AI literacy teaching-guidance claim. Official KKU news/event pages were not used as policy evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.

No central university-wide binding AI policy page was found during this scoped crawl; Graduate School claims are limited to thesis and independent study work, and the AI literacy claim is limited to teaching guidance from the Education and Digital Division page.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
